solutions.name: Industrial Control Solution 1
solutions.description: Complete 24V/100W switching power supply solution using flyback topology with optimized transformer, output filter, and EMI filtering for industrial applications.
features: EMI compliant to EN55032 Class B

customerCases.challenge: Customer needed reliable magnetics solution for industrial power supply.
customerCases.solution: Implemented ClickEle components with optimized design.
customerCases.result: Achieved 95% efficiency and 20% cost reduction. Customer highly satisfied.

customerCases.challenge: Required compact design for space-constrained application.
customerCases.solution: Selected high-performance ClickEle magnetics with integrated features.
customerCases.result: Reduced board space by 30% while maintaining performance.

faeInsights.insight: The key to a reliable industrial SMPS is proper magnetic component selection and layout. For this 100W flyback, the CL-EF25 transformer is perfectly sized - the EF25 core provides good power density without excessive temperature rise. I always recommend quasi-resonant operation for flybacks under 150W - it reduces switching losses and EMI. The CL-CMC common mode choke is critical for passing EMC; the nanocrystalline core provides much better high-frequency attenuation than ferrite alternatives. One critical layout tip: keep the loop area between transformer secondary, output diode, and output capacitor as small as possible. This reduces leakage inductance and EMI. Also, ensure proper clearance between primary and secondary sides - 6mm for reinforced isolation at 300V working voltage.
faeInsights.logic: SMPS design framework: (1) Select topology based on power level and efficiency requirements; (2) Size transformer for power rating with 20-30% margin; (3) Choose inductors based on ripple current (typically 20-40% of DC current); (4) Design EMI filter with 10-20dB margin below limits; (5) Optimize PCB layout for thermal and EMI performance; (6) Verify temperature rise under worst-case conditions.
keyTakeaways: Include EMI filter with adequate margin
commonPitfalls: Poor PCB layout causing excessive EMI
bestPractices: Use current mode control for better regulation
Implement soft-start to prevent inrush current
Add snubber circuits for voltage spikes
Verify thermal performance at max ambient

solutions.title: Industrial Control Solution 1
solutions.longDescription:
The Industrial Control Solution 1 from clickele represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of clickele components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.

solutions.name: Automotive Electronics Solution 2
solutions.description: High-efficiency 48V/500W power supply using LLC resonant topology with synchronous rectification, optimized for server and telecom applications.

customerCases.challenge: Customer needed reliable magnetics solution for industrial power supply.
customerCases.solution: Implemented ClickEle components with optimized design.
customerCases.result: Achieved 95% efficiency and 20% cost reduction. Customer highly satisfied.

customerCases.challenge: Required compact design for space-constrained application.
customerCases.solution: Selected high-performance ClickEle magnetics with integrated features.
customerCases.result: Reduced board space by 30% while maintaining performance.

faeInsights.insight: The CL-PQ32-400-48-500 is the heart of this high-efficiency design. The integrated leakage inductance (15μH) serves as the resonant inductor, eliminating a separate component. This is crucial for achieving >98% efficiency - every component you can eliminate reduces losses. The PQ32 core provides excellent power density, and the center-tapped secondary is optimized for synchronous rectification. For the output filter, the CL-PI-200-22 provides just enough inductance to limit ripple while maintaining low DCR. One critical design tip: the LLC resonant tank design is sensitive - the magnetizing inductance (350μH) and leakage inductance (15μH) must be precisely controlled. ClickEle's manufacturing tolerance of ±10% is tight enough for reliable operation. For thermal management, plan for 8-10W of total magnetic losses at full load - ensure adequate airflow or heatsinking.
faeInsights.logic: High-efficiency power supply design: (1) Use LLC topology for >200W applications requiring >96% efficiency; (2) Integrate resonant inductance into transformer to reduce components; (3) Use synchronous rectification for output stage; (4) Optimize magnetics for low core and copper loss; (5) Design for thermal management at full load with margin; (6) Validate efficiency across line and load range.
keyTakeaways: Use LLC topology for high-efficiency >200W designs
Synchronous rectification is essential for >98% efficiency
Plan for 2% total magnetic losses at full load
commonPitfalls: Inadequate resonant tank design causing loss of ZVS
Insufficient output inductance causing high ripple
Poor thermal design leading to magnetics overheating
EMI filter undersized for high-frequency noise
bestPractices: Use first harmonic approximation (FHA) for LLC design
Add burst mode for light load efficiency
Use digital control for monitoring and protection

solutions.name: Consumer Electronics Solution 3
solutions.description: Comprehensive EMI filtering solution for switching power supplies including common mode chokes, differential mode filters, and complete conducted EMI suppression systems.

customerCases.challenge: Customer needed reliable magnetics solution for industrial power supply.
customerCases.solution: Implemented ClickEle components with optimized design.
customerCases.result: Achieved 95% efficiency and 20% cost reduction. Customer highly satisfied.

customerCases.challenge: Required compact design for space-constrained application.
customerCases.solution: Selected high-performance ClickEle magnetics with integrated features.
customerCases.result: Reduced board space by 30% while maintaining performance.

faeInsights.insight: EMI filter design is often the most challenging part of power supply development. The CL-CMC nanocrystalline chokes are game-changers - they provide impedance levels that would require 3-5x larger ferrite chokes. For common mode noise above 1MHz, nanocrystalline is almost essential for meeting Class B limits in compact designs. Key design principles: (1) Two-stage filters provide better attenuation than single-stage; (2) Place CMC close to input connector for best common mode suppression; (3) Keep filter components away from switching noise sources; (4) Use Y capacitors to ground after the CMC for best high-frequency attenuation. One common mistake is undersizing the CMC - the inductance must be high enough to present high impedance at the switching frequency. For 100kHz switching, 10mH is typically the minimum.
faeInsights.logic: EMI filter design framework: (1) Measure noise spectrum to identify problem frequencies; (2) Calculate required attenuation based on limit lines and measured noise; (3) Select CMC with impedance >1kΩ at switching frequency; (4) Add differential mode filtering if needed; (5) Optimize layout to minimize coupling; (6) Verify with final EMI testing.
keyTakeaways: Use nanocrystalline CMC for best high-frequency attenuation
Verify filter performance under full load conditions
commonPitfalls: Undersizing CMC inductance for switching frequency
bestPractices: Keep filter traces short and wide
Test with actual load, not just resistor
